species: Steller Sea Lion -- adult bull, apex roar
sound_type: Adult bull apex roar at haul-out: maximum-amplitude territorial vocalization from a mature male (800-1,000 kg); a deep resonant sustained roar (80-500 Hz primary energy, harmonics to 2,000 Hz); produced during haul-out territorial contests and at breeding rookeries during peak season; the loudest individual pinniped vocalization in the PNW engine by absolute sound pressure level; the largest otariid in the world at maximum acoustic output
ecosystem: Rocky offshore islets, sea stacks, and exposed headlands -- high-energy Pacific coastline; Steller Sea Lions require hauled-out sites with tidal access, space for large body-mass animals, and proximity to productive marine foraging habitat; Washington State: Carroll Island (off Cape Flattery), Seal Rocks, scattered haul-out sites along the outer Washington coast; the rookery acoustic context (multiple bulls simultaneously) is distinct from isolated haul-out (single bull); this entry documents the single apex bull roar
season: Year-round at haul-out sites. Peak acoustic intensity: breeding season (May-July) when bulls are in active territorial contest at rookeries. Non-breeding haul-out territorial roaring year-round at maintained sites. Washington State haul-out activity peaks in non-breeding season (August-April) as animals move south from Alaskan breeding rookeries.
conservation_status: Least Concern (IUCN) for Western population (Alaska, Russia). Eastern population (Oregon, Washington, California): listed as Threatened under the US Endangered Species Act. Washington State population recovering. The eastern population decline (1970s-2000s) was one of the most significant Pacific marine mammal population events; recovery is ongoing.
milestone: FIRST STELLER SEA LION ENTRY IN ENGINE. E=8. The largest otariid in the world: adult bulls 800-1,000 kg, 2.5-3.2m body length. Loudest individual pinniped vocalization in the PNW by absolute sound pressure level. Body mass as vocalization mass: the roar of a 1,000 kg animal is fundamentally different from any smaller pinniped call. POST-SUMMIT ENERGY GRADIENT: E=8 at degree 316 -- one degree after E=7 (degree 315), the gradient steps back up.

coordinates.description: Cape Flattery / Carroll Island area: westernmost point of contiguous US, documented Steller Sea Lion haul-out. Seal Rocks (off Olympic Peninsula coast). Oregon coast rookeries (Rogue Reef, Orford Reef) are the closest breeding rookeries to Washington State. Alaska breeding rookeries (Marmot Island, Forrester Island) are the source population for Washington winter haul-out animals.

acoustic_data.vocalization_type: Adult bull roar: deep resonant vocalization produced from the chest and larynx of a 800-1,000 kg animal. Low-frequency foundation (80-200 Hz), mid-frequency harmonics (200-500 Hz primary energy), upper harmonics (500-2,000 Hz). Sustained for 2-6 seconds. May be repeated in sequences during territorial contest. Audible at 500m+ over open water in calm conditions.
acoustic_data.mechanics: Otariid vocalization: the eared seals (Otariidae, distinguished from true seals Phocidae by external ear flaps and rotating rear flippers) produce vocalizations primarily through laryngeal vibration with oral resonance. The Steller Sea Lion's large body mass provides extraordinary resonating space: a 1,000 kg animal has proportionally larger chest cavity and longer vocal tract than any smaller pinniped. The roar is physically generated by mass.
acoustic_data.frequency_range: Primary energy 80-500 Hz. Harmonics to 2,000 Hz. Low-frequency dominance: the 80-200 Hz fundamental is what carries over distance at sea. The roar is felt before it is heard at close range (body-vibration threshold for 80 Hz at high amplitude).
acoustic_data.duration: Individual roar: 2-6 seconds. Sequence during territorial contest: 10-30 roars over 5-15 minutes. Peak rookery activity: near-continuous acoustic output from multiple bulls for hours during breeding season. This entry documents the single bull apex roar (2-6 seconds, maximum amplitude single vocalization).
acoustic_data.e8_calibration: E=8: Four factors: (1) absolute sound pressure level: adult bull Steller Sea Lion roar is the loudest individual pinniped vocalization in the Pacific Northwest by sound pressure level; estimated 100-115 dB at 1m (among highest of any marine mammal in the engine's range); (2) body mass as acoustic mass: 1,000 kg producing 80-500 Hz primary -- the low-frequency resonance is physically generated by the largest eared seal body in the world; (3) behavioral context: apex territorial vocalization from an animal at the top of the Pacific coast food web (with Killer Whale as only predator); (4) population recovery significance: Eastern Steller Sea Lion is Threatened; each documented haul-out is ecologically significant. Combined: E=8.

nodes.title: First Steller Sea Lion Entry -- Largest Otariid, Loudest Pinniped Roar
nodes.description: Eumetopias jubatus: the world's largest eared seal. Adult bulls 800-1,000 kg, 2.5-3.2m body length. The Steller Sea Lion bull apex roar is the loudest individual pinniped vocalization in the Pacific Northwest by absolute sound pressure level. First entry: documented at E=8.

nodes.title: Body Mass as Acoustic Mass -- E=8 Through Physical Size
nodes.description: The Steller Sea Lion's E=8 is generated by mass. A 1,000 kg animal has proportionally large chest cavity, long vocal tract, and enormous resonating space. The 80-500 Hz primary frequency range is the natural resonant frequency of these anatomical structures at this body scale. E=8 is not achieved through behavioral intensity alone; it is physically manufactured by the animal's mass. The acoustic event is inevitable given the body size.

nodes.title: Eastern Steller Sea Lion -- Threatened, Recovering
nodes.description: The Eastern Steller Sea Lion population (Oregon, Washington, California) was listed as Threatened under the US Endangered Species Act after population declines in the 1970s-2000s. Recovery is ongoing. Washington State haul-out sites (Carroll Island, Seal Rocks) are monitoring locations for this recovery. The engine's acoustic documentation intersects with conservation monitoring: to hear a Steller Sea Lion bull apex roar at a Washington haul-out is to document a recovering Threatened species.

nodes.title: Post-Summit Gradient Uptick -- E=8 Following E=7
nodes.description: The post-Summit SPS gradient: E=9 (311-313), E=8 (314), E=7 (315), E=8 (316). Degree 316 steps back up one unit from degree 315's E=7. The gradient is not a simple monotone descent; it has local variation. The Steller Sea Lion's E=8 represents the mass-based acoustic anchor in the post-Summit zone that interrupts the descent.

nodes.title: Gruntruck -- Steller Sea Lion Equivalence
nodes.description: Gruntruck E=8: the metal floor is maintained by body weight -- Ben McMillan's full-chest baritone, Niemeyer's heavy riff architecture, the Roadrunner Records metal genealogy. Mass as the E=8 generator on the S36 track. Steller Sea Lion E=8: the roar is generated by 1,000 kg of body mass. Both E=8 through the weight of the physical instrument (body mass) rather than through behavioral intensity or frequency complexity. The degree-316 pairing is the mass pairing.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Mass pairing: Gruntruck E=8 through metal body weight (McMillan baritone, heavy riff, Roadrunner genealogy) + Steller Sea Lion E=8 through physical body mass (1,000 kg, 80-500 Hz roar). Both E=8 generated by mass.

E=7 preceding E=8. The gradient steps back up: Varied Thrush (minimalist, singular, atmospheric, E=7) vs. Steller Sea Lion (maximum mass, maximum volume, E=8). Opposite ends of the SPS expression range in consecutive degrees.

Marine mammal thread: Orca (E=9, maximum combined cetacean intelligence + pack vocalizing) -> Steller Sea Lion (E=8, maximum single pinniped volume). Marine mammals hold the upper energy register in the post-Summit zone: Orca (311), Storm (312), Steller (316).

Multiple bulls at breeding rookery = higher combined acoustic density. Future entry at E=9 potential for full rookery documentation. Degree 316 documents the single bull apex; the rookery collective event is a future entry.
